meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome design limitations. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first portfolio project.
content.answer_block: You don't need a designer to build beautiful apps. By leveraging Git-integrated AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can translate natural language into production-ready UI components. This workflow automates design systems, allowing you to focus on logic while the AI handles the aesthetic architecture of your application.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, code logic ready, but the UI looks like a relic from 1995. The 'no design skills' wall is the single biggest barrier between a student project and a portfolio-ready SaaS application. It’s the difference between a tool that works and a product that sells.

But the era of manual CSS tweaking is over. By integrating AI-driven workflows into your Git lifecycle, you stop being a designer and start being an architect. We are going to bridge the gap between your logic and a high-end interface using Antigravity and Claude Code, turning your raw code into a polished, professional product.

steps.title: Initialize the Design Context
steps.description: Establish a consistent visual identity by defining a theme file before writing components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modern, minimalist Tailwind CSS configuration file focusing on a professional SaaS palette: #0f172a primary and #f8fafc background.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create tailwind.config.js with a refined color palette and typography scale.'

steps.title: Component Architecture Setup
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold a component library that enforces design consistency across your repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a reusable Button and Card component using React and Tailwind, ensuring accessibility and hover states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create src/components/ui/Button.tsx with variants for primary and ghost styles.'

steps.title: Git-Driven UI Iteration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to apply design changes directly through Git commits to maintain version history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the landing page layout to a responsive two-column grid using CSS Flexbox.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'Implement responsive grid layout for the landing page'

steps.title: Automated Visual Debugging
steps.description: Let the AI identify layout shifts and alignment issues within your Git branches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current UI for common alignment errors and suggest fixes for mobile responsiveness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review 'Check for visual inconsistencies and suggest refactors for mobile view'

steps.title: Final Polish & Deployment Prep
steps.description: Use AI to finalize documentation and ensure your design system is documented for future scaling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean README.md file that explains the design system architecture used in this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a professional README.md that highlights the UI architecture and design choices.'

pro_tips: Always commit your design changes in atomic, feature-specific branches to allow for easy reverts if the AI generates a layout that breaks your logic.
Use Claude Code's 'review' feature on every pull request to catch accessibility (a11y) issues you might have missed as a solo developer.
Define your brand variables (colors, spacing, font) in a single JSON schema before starting; it acts as the 'source of truth' for the AI.
Treat your UI code as data—use AI to generate unit tests for your design components to ensure they stay consistent as your app grows.
If the AI produces generic code, provide it with a specific 'Design System' reference document (like a Tailwind UI snippet) as context.
content.next_steps: After completing your first UI build, push your code to GitHub and request a code review from the AI Builder community to iterate on your architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design anxiety keep your best ideas in the draft folder. You have the tools, the logic, and the workflow to ship professional software today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
